words.gloss: to admit, confess, claim as one's own (distinct from the pronoun aku, 'I/me')
to raise, care for (a child) (bound root)
bastard, scoundrel, jerk (vulgar/mild curse depending on context)
will be, prospective, future (colloquial, e.g. bakal calon = prospective candidate)
stand on one's own two feet
welt; long scar on the skin from a whip (archaic, KBBI); also a bare verb: to smooth a surface by rubbing (cloth, wood)
free, for free, free of charge
finished, used up, gone; out of
Henry (a given name); also the SI unit of electrical inductance
to contain, carry (a child), harbor (bound root)
to be hit by, affected by; to touch, come into contact with
to drive, to ride (a vehicle) (bound root)
i came, i saw, i conquered
I saw, I looked (colloq. contraction of aku + lihat)
i came, i saw, i conquered
in three days, two days after tomorrow
visible, apparent; to appear (colloquial variant of tampak)
most (superlative marker); to turn, face (bound root, as in berpaling)
damn it, fuck off, to hell with
dying, in the throes of death
to have an affair, cheat (bound root)
attention, on your mark, get set, go, prepared
to collect payment, demand payment (bound root)
to appear, perform, show up (bound root)
to ride along, lodge, hitch a ride (bound root)
